Projects.title: LifeLink
desc: LifeLink is a blood donation mobile application designed to help people quickly find blood donors and blood banks during emergencies. The project was inspired by the difficulties a friend faced while arranging blood during a critical situation.

The design process included user research, user flows, paper wireframes, low-fidelity wireframes, and high-fidelity prototypes. The goal was to simplify the process of finding donors, submitting blood requests, and connecting with nearby blood banks.
The final prototype focused on accessibility, ease of navigation, and reducing the time required for users to respond to urgent blood requests.

caseStudy.title: The Problem
content: During medical emergencies, finding blood donors quickly can be difficult. Existing methods often rely on personal networks, social media posts, and manual coordination, leading to delays when time is critical.
The goal of LifeLink was to create a centralized platform that enables users to locate blood donors, discover nearby blood banks, and respond to urgent requests with minimal friction.

caseStudy.title: User Journey Map
caseStudy.image: /image/case studies/life link new/Life link user journey map.svg

caseStudy.title: Personalized Home Dashboard
caseStudy.content: The home screen provides immediate access to urgent requests, donation actions, and nearby resources while maintaining a simple and accessible navigation structure.

caseStudy.title: Donation Confirmation Flow
caseStudy.content: The donation flow helps users verify eligibility and review request information before confirming their participation.

caseStudy.title: Blood Bank Discovery
caseStudy.content: Users can search nearby blood banks and access relevant information when immediate assistance is required.

caseStudy.title: Profile & Impact Tracking
caseStudy.content: The profile section enables users to manage personal information, view donation history, and track their contributions.

caseStudy.title: Reflection
content: This project strengthened my understanding of designing for high-pressure situations where users need information quickly and decisions must be made with confidence.
Future improvements include real-time donor matching, location-based notifications, blood inventory integration, and emergency alert systems.

Projects.title: Fam Manager
desc: Fam Manager is a family management platform designed to help families organize important documents, schedules, and events in one secure place. The idea originated from personal challenges in managing family records and appointments.
The project involved user research, information architecture, paper wireframes, low-fidelity wireframes, mobile and web interface design, and high-fidelity prototypes.
The final solution enables families to securely store documents, manage schedules, organize events, and access important information through an intuitive user experience.

caseStudy.title: The Problem
content: Families often struggle to keep important documents, schedules, and records organized across multiple locations and platforms.
The goal of Fam Manager was to create a centralized solution that enables families to securely manage documents, appointments, events, and essential information in one place.

caseStudy.title: User Journey Map
caseStudy.image: /image/case studies/fam manager new/user journey fam manager.svg

caseStudy.title: Core Experience
images: /image/case studies/fam manager new/landing page fam manager.png

caseStudy.title: Document Dashboard
caseStudy.content: The dashboard serves as the central hub where families can quickly access and organize important records.

caseStudy.title: Personal Documents
caseStudy.content: Users can securely store personal documents such as identification cards, certificates, and other essential records.

caseStudy.title: Government Documents
caseStudy.content: Government-issued records are categorized separately to improve discoverability and organization.

caseStudy.title: Medical Records
caseStudy.content: Medical documents are organized in a dedicated section, making them easily accessible during appointments and emergencies.

caseStudy.title: Family Documents
caseStudy.content: Shared family records can be securely stored and accessed by family members when needed.

caseStudy.title: Add New Documents
caseStudy.content: A simple upload workflow allows users to add and categorize documents with minimal effort.

caseStudy.title: Event Management
caseStudy.content: Families can manage appointments, reminders, and important events through a centralized scheduling experience.

caseStudy.title: Create Events
caseStudy.content: Users can quickly create and organize new events, ensuring family schedules remain coordinated.

caseStudy.title: Reflection
content: This project strengthened my understanding of information architecture and designing systems that manage large amounts of personal information.
Future improvements include cloud synchronization, family permissions, document sharing, automated reminders, and smart notifications.

Projects.title: AgroVision
desc: AgroVision is a precision farming platform designed to help farmers make informed decisions using soil, weather, and crop health data. The project focused on simplifying complex agricultural information into a clear and actionable user experience.

The platform integrates GIS data, Google Earth Engine, NDVI analysis, and machine learning techniques to provide crop health monitoring, environmental insights, and farm management support through interactive dashboards.

The project was published at IEEE ICCAMS 2025 and demonstrates the application of AI-driven agricultural analytics, geospatial technologies, and user-centered design to support data-informed farming decisions.

caseStudy.title: The Problem
content: Modern farming generates large amounts of environmental and crop-related data, but most farmers lack accessible tools to convert that information into actionable insights.
The goal of AgroVision was to create a platform that combines GIS, NDVI analysis, weather information, and machine learning into a single intuitive dashboard.

caseStudy.title: Select your coordinates using the map or enter the values manually
Impact
content: AgroVision demonstrates how AI, geospatial technologies, and data visualization can be combined to support precision agriculture.
The project was published at IEEE ICCAMS 2025 and highlights the value of user-centered design in agricultural technology solutions.
